description_long: This house features a symmetrical front facade with two prominent entryways, each flanked by white columns and accessed via short steps. The exterior is clad in horizontal gray siding, and the roof is a dark, steeply pitched hip style with dormer-like projections on either side. Large, multi-pane windows are arranged in neat rows on both floors, providing ample natural light. The front yard is landscaped with geometric pathways, planters, and spherical decorative elements. Metal railings line the entry steps, adding a touch of formality to the entrance. The overall design suggests a blend of Colonial and modern suburban influences, with a focus on balance and curb appeal.
description: A two-story house with a symmetrical facade, gray siding, dark hip roof, and formal dual entryways accented by columns and landscaping.
description_view_0: Front view showing dual entryways with columns, symmetrical windows, landscaped paths, and decorative spheres.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the house, additional windows, and the extension of the roof and siding.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a continuous row of windows and the full expanse of the gray siding and roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with more windows and a clear look at the side extension and metal railings.
attribute_entryway_design: Dual front doors with white columns, short steps, metal railings, and decorative landscaping elements.
